betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Lead§f

§aA soft, toxic metal for radiation shielding, soldering, and ammunition.§f

Lead ore is found between Y 5–35 (6 veins/chunk). Smelt it in a basic furnace. Lead nuggets also appear as decay products from many reactor fuels.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Lead Anvil§f (alternative to iron anvil)
§b• Soldering§f (circuit production)
§b• Ammunition§f
§b• Radiation protection§f (hazmat suits, reactor shielding)
§b• Reactor components§f (Watz, breeding reactor)

§eTip§f: Always keep lead stocked — it's cheap and you'll need it for soldering once you reach electronics.
